title: Smoky Savory Moroccan Butternut Squash Kefta Tagine
description: A garlicky and gooey Moroccan dish featuring butternut squash, made in a tandoor style with Mediterranean friendly flavors, in a kefta tagine form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, chopped onion, minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, if using, or shape into small balls or patties.
In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat the olive oil over medium high heat.
Add the lamb keftas to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cubed butternut squash to the pot, and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken broth to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the squash is tender and the keftas are cooked through.
Serve the keftas and squash hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
